name: Natural Gas / LNG
summary: Day 69 Thursday. **CHENIERE LNG -3.0% TO $261.42 ON LNG-SCARCITY PREMIUM UNWIND + Q1 EARNINGS RESIDUAL.** TTF crashed -6.8% May 6 to ~€47.4/MWh on MOU scoop — pricing pause as plausibly durable. The Cheniere -3.0% specific: LNG-scarcity premium (Qatar Ras Laffan damage thesis) being partially repriced as European TTF gas crashed; Cheniere reported Q1 today with modest miss on guidance forward. Henry Hub $2.72/MMBtu (-0.5% 1D, -0.3% 1M, -13.4% 3M) — non-crisis driver dominates; cold-snap residual / spring transition. Structural drivers persist: Hormuz functionally closed Day 69 but kinetic-war probability compressed; Qatar Ras Laffan Trains 4/6 (12.8 mtpa, ~17% Qatar LNG) still 3-5yr recovery. **Reversibility:** if MOU signs, Cheniere could give back another 5-10% on full peace pricing; if Iran rejects, Cheniere bounces back toward $275-280 within 48hr on premium re-pricing. The MOU framework is the marginal pricing factor — Qatar Ras Laffan reopening is in the 'lift Hormuz blockades' provision but physical recovery is years out regardless of diplomatic resolution.

current.delta_3m: HH -13.4%; TTF est +35% vs pre-crisis; Cheniere n/a

recent_signals.headline: BRANCH 3 → CHENIERE EUROPEAN-ENERGY-EMERGENCY PREMIUM COMPRESSION; HH +6.8% NON-CRISIS

historical_summaries.summary: 4 signals. QatarEnergy halted LNG production at Ras Laffan (world's largest LNG facility, ~20% of global supply) after attacks | Iranian missiles damaged LNG Trains 4 & 6 at Ras Laffan | European TTF surged 70% in March to EUR54
18 signals. Eurozone inflation surged to 2 | CONFIRMED: European TTF gas at EUR 47 | CONFIRMED: Heating oil +3
